mutations.change: Rename a reviewed TypeScript client module to JavaScript or add an untyped JavaScript source module.
mutations.reason: The client task explicitly requires strict TypeScript acceptance across its complete authored source inventory.

mutations.change: Add a TypeScript suppression directive or any-typed escape hatch around payload validation.
mutations.reason: Suppression could conceal malformed transport and DOM state paths that must fail during compilation or lint.

mutations.change: Import a UI framework, game engine, WAD parser, renderer package, Node builtin, or remote module.
mutations.reason: The browser must remain a thin presentation client and may not acquire a second engine or expanded dependency surface.

mutations.change: Add a gameplay, world, entity, renderer, physics, networking-helper, analytics, or worker module.
mutations.reason: Only the seven named concerns plus one bootstrap module are inside the reviewed thin-client boundary.

mutations.change: Show a landing, loading, consent, marketing, or click-to-start page before presenting the canvas.
mutations.reason: The game must be served immediately and browser audio permission cannot become a general entry gate.

mutations.change: Change intrinsic canvas width or height, or substitute a CSS-scaled non-320-by-200 backing buffer.
mutations.reason: Canonical game pixels and raw canvas hashes require an exact 320 by 200 intrinsic drawing surface.

mutations.change: Decode database column runs as row-major runs or transpose the resulting palette index plane.
mutations.reason: The transport is column RLE while canvas ImageData is row-major, so orientation conversion is explicit.

mutations.change: Accept a column that covers only 199 rows and leave the final pixel implicit or stale.
mutations.reason: Every frame must replace exactly 64,000 pixels with no clipping, omission, or prior-frame residue.

mutations.change: Offset every palette lookup by one entry or swap the red and blue output channels.
mutations.reason: Raw getImageData hashes bind exact palette indexing, channel order, alpha bytes, and pixel ordering.

mutations.change: Drop, duplicate, retain, or alpha-clear one pixel while blitting a complete decoded frame.
mutations.reason: Exact canvas byte identity detects even a one-pixel client presentation corruption.

mutations.change: Enable interpolated image rendering or distort the canvas away from the 8-to-5 aspect ratio.
mutations.reason: The low-resolution canonical frame must remain crisp and geometrically undistorted at viewport scale.

mutations.change: Overlay navigation, status text, touch buttons, branding, or instructions on the desktop canvas.
mutations.reason: The immediate full-viewport game canvas is the experience and no presentation element may obscure it.

mutations.change: Shrink a visible mobile touch target below forty CSS pixels or place it outside the viewport.
mutations.reason: Minimal icons still must be reachable and safe to operate on portrait and landscape touch screens.

mutations.change: Allow touch controls to overlap one another, the canonical canvas, or create document scrolling.
mutations.reason: Responsive presentation must preserve the full game image and unambiguous touch interaction.

mutations.change: Fail to release held keyboard state on keyup, blur, or document visibility loss.
mutations.reason: A stuck movement or fire control continues server commands after the physical interaction has ended.

mutations.change: Reuse, gap, fractionally encode, or locally predict the public STEP command sequence.
mutations.reason: Only exact consecutive integer commands may advance the authoritative database simulation.

mutations.change: Ignore pointerup, pointercancel, capture loss, or slide-away and retain an active mobile control.
mutations.reason: All touch termination paths must clear held server input without creating synthetic keyboard state.

mutations.change: Emit duplicate fire or use commands from both pointer and compatibility click events.
mutations.reason: One physical action must have one deterministic effect and must not reorder simultaneous controls.

mutations.change: Create or resume audible playback before the first user gesture or bypass browser audio policy.
mutations.reason: Audio presentation must honor the browser gesture boundary without delaying the visual game bootstrap.

mutations.change: Schedule a server audio tuple twice, ignore gain or pan, or continue scheduling while muted.
mutations.reason: The client presents authoritative events once and cannot synthesize or duplicate gameplay audio.

mutations.change: Request a remote font, image, script, analytics endpoint, CDN, or non-AutoREST dynamic route.
mutations.reason: The production client must be self-contained and all dynamic traffic stays on the reviewed API surface.

mutations.change: Catch malformed gzip, JSON, frame, palette, or request failures and continue with a blank frame.
mutations.reason: A transport or decode error is an observable failure, never a successful empty or stale game frame.

mutations.change: Advance movement, animation, physics, tic, RNG, entity, or predicted frame state in the browser.
mutations.reason: Presentation state is deliberately narrow and Oracle remains the sole authoritative game engine.

mutations.change: Alter a frozen fixture, reference, test id, mutation, runner, browser probe, or approved ancestry pointer.
mutations.reason: Evaluator freezing fixes the independent target before production implementation starts.
